title: ledum groenlandicum var. compactum
intro: Ledum groenlandicum var. compactum, also known as compact Labrador tea, is a subspecies of the Labrador tea plant, a low-growing evergreen shrub native to northern regions of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which aid in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Ledum groenlandicum var. compactum have been found to provide health benefits such as soothing respiratory issues, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water, offering a natural remedy for various health concerns.
